rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. EVEREST INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 30, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF REAL ENTERPRISES, LLC's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Denied, without prejudice. Counsel failed to proffer his declaration in support of the motion on Judicial Council form MC-052, which is mandatory. "The motion to be relieved as counsel must be accompanied by a declaration on the Declaration in Support of Attorney's Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel--Civil (form MC-052)." (CRC 3.1362(c).) Further, the Hamrick Declaration does not contemplate all of the information required on Form MC-052. (CRC 3.1362(d).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN SIERRA VS. AMBASSADOR PRIVATE SECURITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Or Return Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 30, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT EDD OZARD's Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Or Return Of Summons. Granted. CCP § 583.250 provides that dismissal of a defendant not served within three years after the action is commenced is mandatory. An action is commenced against a Doe defendant at the date the complaint is filed, not the date of the filing of the Doe amendment. (See Rios v. Torvald Klaveness (1969) 2 Cal.App.3d 1077, 1082; Taliaferro v. Riddle (1959) 167 Cal.App.2d 567, 659.) The Complaint was filed in this action on August 6, 2009. Defendant Edd Ozard was not served until September 5, 2013, beyond the three year period. Plaintiff fails to meet his burden of establishing any statutory exception to this rule. (See CCP §583.250(b)).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION:$590.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 30, 2013, Line 2 - PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Order Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion Granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROZINA GEBREMEDHIN et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 30, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. (CCP § 437c.) No opposition filed. Defendant CPMC has demonstrated that it did not breach any duty to Ms. Gebremedhin. (UMF nos. 7 - 10.) " ' "The standard of care against which the acts of a physician are to be measured is a matter peculiarly within the knowledge of experts; it presents the basic issue in a malpractice action and can only be proved by their testimony [citations], unless the conduct required by the particular circumstances is within the common knowledge of the layman." [Citations.]' [Citations.]" (Flowers v. Torrance Mem'l Hosp. Med. Ctr. (1994) 8 Cal.4th 992, 1001.) Defendant CPMC has demonstrated that, to a reasonable medical probability, the care and treatment provided by Defendant CPMC did not cause any injury to Ms. Gebremedhin. (UMF no. 11.) "The law is well settled that in a personal injury action causation must be proven within a reasonable medical probability based [on] competent expert testimony. Mere possibility alone is insufficient to establish a prima facie case." (Jennings v. Palomar Pomerado Health Systems, Inc. (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 1108, 1118.) Mr. Hadera cannot prevail on his loss of consortium claim that is contingent on Ms. Bebremedhin's claims. "[A]n unsuccessful personal injury suit by the physically injured spouse acts as an estoppel that bars the spouse who would claim damages for loss of consortium." (Meighan v. Shore (1995) 34 Cal.App.4th 1025, 1034-35. See UMF no. 15) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: INTEGRATED CLEANING SOLUTIONS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. WILLIAM JOHNSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 30, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM JOHNSON AND BAY CLEANING SOLUTIONS, LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. William Johnson's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the First Cause of Action for breach of written contract is granted without leave to amend. No opposition was filed. The anti-solicitation clause violates California Business & Professions Code § 16600. See Edwards v. Arthur Anderson LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, 948; Dowell v. Pacesetter (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 564. rulings.Case Title: ANDRES MURGUIA VS. ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Allstate To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documetns, Set One.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 30, 2013, Line 11 - PLAINTIFF ANDRES MURGUIA's Motion To Compel Allstate To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documetns, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: It appears from the parties' briefs that the only remaining items in dispute on this motion are Plaintiffs' requests relating to (a) Defendant's marketing/advertisements (Request Nos.5-7) and (b) complaints and verdicts/judgments against Defendant (Request Nos. 14 and 15). The motion is denied as to both categories. The marketing/advertising requests are beyond the scope of permissible discovery as this case does not involve claims of, for example, false advertising or fraud in the inducement. Rather, it concerns the denial of insurance benefits under a homeowners policy issued to Plaintiff. The complaints and verdicts/judgments sought under Requests Nos. 14 and 15 are not the type of "pattern and practice" discovery contemplated by the Colonial Life decision. Furthermore, the requests, by their terms, are so broad that they cannot reasonably be construed as seeking discovery that is relevant to the subject matter of this action or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TINA GALLEGOS et al VS. GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE HIGHWAY AND TRANSPORTATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Pltff'S First Cause Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 30, 2013, Line 26, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE HIGHWAY AND TRANSPORTATION's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiff'S First Cause Of Action. Denied. The requests for judicial notice are granted. As to Government Code §815.6, Plaintiffs plead common carrier law as the statute or regulation giving rise to the mandatory duty. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC") 5.) This is sufficient. The Court will not consider the argument raised for the first time in reply that there is no common carrier liability as a matter of law. As to Government Code § 815.4, the FAC at 2 pleads that defendants were "servants" acting within the "scope of their service." This is sufficient to state a cause of action against an independent contractor. As to § 835, the FAC at 6 pleads that Defendant "built" and "maintained" the gates in an "unsafe and dangerous condition," which is sufficient to state a cause of action for premises liability. The Court also finds that the FAC is predicated on the same matters set forth in the claim submitted to Defendant pursuant to Government Code § 910(f). The Court disregards the declarations filed in connection with this motion; the grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings "shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice." (CCP §438(d).) Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
